exam.name: Biology: Content Knowledge
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for future educators with syllabus-mapped coverage built directly from official certification blueprints.
exam.hero_headline: Pass Praxis 5236 with biology content mapped to the official objectives
exam.hero_subheadline: Study cell biology, genetics, evolution, organismal biology, ecology, and the nature of science in focused 15-minute topics aligned to the ETS Study Companion.
exam.companion_problem: Most Praxis biology prep either stays shallow across vast domains like genetics and ecology or piles facts without the cross-topic structure and application the exam expects.
exam.companion_solution: We translate the official ETS syllabus into a full learning map with topic-sized notes, explicit domain boundaries, and retention-focused structure covering all five major biology content categories.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring secondary biology teacher preparing for selected-response items spanning the nature of science, cell biology, genetics, evolution, diversity of life, and ecology. Benefits from diagrams, biological processes, and science and engineering practices.

sections.title: Nature and Impact of Science and Engineering
sections.description: Fundamental principles of scientific inquiry, experimental design, laboratory procedures, engineering practices, and the impact of science on society.

topics.title: Nature of Scientific Knowledge
topics.description: Understanding how scientific knowledge is generated through evidence, modeling, and interdisciplinary approaches.
learning_objectives: Explain how observations and experiments provide evidence for scientific knowledge.
Differentiate between scientific laws, theories, and hypotheses.
Describe the interdisciplinary nature of science and how models explain natural phenomena.

topics.title: Experimental Design and Analysis
topics.description: Principles of designing experiments, collecting data, and performing error analysis.
learning_objectives: Identify variables, formulate hypotheses, and plan data collection strategies.
Apply standard units, dimensional analysis, and significant figures in data reporting.
Identify sources of experimental error and extrapolate valid conclusions from data.

topics.title: Laboratory Procedures and Safety
topics.description: Safe use of laboratory equipment, materials preparation, and emergency procedures.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate understanding of the proper preparation of biological and chemical materials.
Identify appropriate storage, handling, and disposal methods for lab chemicals.
Describe standard safety and emergency procedures in a science laboratory.

topics.title: Engineering Design Process
topics.description: Applying the engineering design cycle to identify problems and optimize solutions.
learning_objectives: Define engineering problems including success criteria and constraints.
Evaluate proposed design solutions and systematic modifications.
Explain the interdependence and feedback loop between science and engineering discoveries.

topics.title: Human Impacts on the Environment
topics.description: Evaluating how human activity and technology affect ecosystems and society.
learning_objectives: Analyze sources and impacts of air/water pollution and greenhouse gases.
Discuss the consequences of natural disasters, resource extraction, and agriculture.
Evaluate conservation efforts and the sustainable management of renewable energy.

topics.title: Science in Health and Agriculture
topics.description: Applications of biology in epidemiology, biotechnology, and medical technology.
learning_objectives: Examine principles of epidemiology, disease spread, and vaccine development.
Describe agricultural applications of biotechnology like GMOs.
Analyze ethical concerns regarding medical technologies, stem cells, and genetic cloning.

sections.title: Cell Biology: Cell Structure and Function
sections.description: Biochemistry, thermodynamics, organelle function, membrane transport, and the cell cycle.

topics.title: Biological Molecules and Water
topics.description: Chemical structures of major macromolecules and the unique properties of water.
learning_objectives: Explain chemical bonding and the distinction between organic and inorganic molecules.
Describe the properties of water based on its molecular structure and hydrogen bonding.
Identify the structures and functions of carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids.

topics.title: Thermodynamics and Metabolism
topics.description: Physical principles governing biological processes, energy flow, and redox reactions.
learning_objectives: Apply the laws of thermodynamics to biological systems.
Distinguish between anabolic and catabolic metabolic pathways.
Explain the role of chemical gradients and reduction-oxidation reactions in cellular metabolism.

topics.title: Enzyme Structure and Kinetics
topics.description: The function of enzymes, active sites, and factors affecting enzyme kinetics.
learning_objectives: Describe how active site structure enables specific substrate binding.
Analyze the energy profile of a reaction with and without an enzyme.
Explain the effects of temperature, pH, competitive inhibitors, and noncompetitive inhibitors on enzyme activity.

topics.title: Cellular Respiration
topics.description: Biochemical pathways of aerobic and anaerobic cellular respiration.
learning_objectives: Describe the structure and function of ATP in cellular processes.
Detail the stages of aerobic respiration: glycolysis, the citric acid cycle, and oxidative phosphorylation.
Compare aerobic respiration with anaerobic fermentation processes.

topics.title: Photosynthesis
topics.description: The process of photosynthesis, light-dependent reactions, and the Calvin-Benson cycle.
learning_objectives: Identify the cellular locations and structures involved in photosynthesis.
Explain the roles of photosystems and electron transport in generating energy carriers.
Describe the Calvin-Benson cycle and compare photosynthesis to chemosynthesis.

topics.title: Cell Theory and Organization
topics.description: Foundational characteristics of living things, cell theory, and basic cell types.
learning_objectives: State the components of the cell theory and define characteristics of living versus nonliving things.
Explain how cells maintain homeostasis and respond to environmental changes.
Differentiate between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell organization.

topics.title: Organelles and Extracellular Matrix
topics.description: Structures and roles of cellular organelles and the cytoskeleton.
learning_objectives: Compare and contrast the structural components of plant and animal cells.
Describe the functions of membrane-bound organelles and ribosomes.
Explain the roles of the cytoskeleton and extracellular matrix in cellular support and movement.

topics.title: Cell Membrane and Transport
topics.description: Structure of the plasma membrane and mechanisms of cellular transport.
learning_objectives: Explain the fluid mosaic model and the function of the phospholipid bilayer.
Differentiate between active transport, passive transport, simple diffusion, and facilitated diffusion.
Analyze water movement concepts, including osmolarity, water potential, exocytosis, and endocytosis.

topics.title: Cell Communication
topics.description: How cells receive, process, and respond to external signals.
learning_objectives: Describe the roles of cell surface proteins and signaling molecules.
Outline the steps of a typical signal transduction pathway.
Explain how negative and positive feedback mechanisms regulate cellular responses.

topics.title: Cell Cycle and Division
topics.description: Stages of eukaryotic cell division, mitosis, and cell cycle regulation.
learning_objectives: Identify the stages and checkpoints of the cell cycle.
Describe the functions, stages, and results of mitosis and cytokinesis.
Explain how the failure of cell cycle checkpoints can lead to unregulated cell proliferation and cancer.

sections.title: Genetics and Evolution
sections.description: Molecular genetics, inheritance patterns, population genetics, and evolutionary theory.

topics.title: Nucleic Acids and Chromosomes
topics.description: The molecular structure of DNA, RNA, and eukaryotic chromosomes.
learning_objectives: Describe the sugar-phosphate backbone and complementary base pairing of nucleic acids.
Compare and contrast the structure and function of DNA versus RNA.
Explain chromosome structure, including the role of nucleosomes and telomeres.

topics.title: DNA Replication and Transcription
topics.description: The central dogma of molecular biology and the synthesis of DNA and RNA.
learning_objectives: Outline the central dogma of molecular biology.
Describe the continuous and discontinuous synthesis processes of DNA replication.
Explain the steps of RNA transcription and pre-mRNA processing in eukaryotes.

topics.title: Translation and Gene Regulation
topics.description: Protein synthesis mechanics and the regulation of gene expression.
learning_objectives: Explain the roles of mRNA, tRNA, rRNA, and ribosomes in translation.
Utilize a genetic code chart to determine amino acid sequences.
Describe gene regulation mechanisms including promoters, enhancers, transcription factors, and posttranslational controls.

topics.title: Mutations and Biotechnology
topics.description: Causes and consequences of genetic mutations and modern biotechnology techniques.
learning_objectives: Identify types and causes of mutations, including substitutions, frameshifts, and chromosomal changes.
Explain the significance of somatic versus germ-line mutations and resultant disorders.
Describe laboratory techniques such as gel electrophoresis, PCR, genome sequencing, and gene therapy.

topics.title: Mendelian Genetics
topics.description: Core principles of Mendelian inheritance and genetic crosses.
learning_objectives: Define dominant and recessive alleles and their phenotypic expressions.
Apply the laws of segregation and independent assortment using monohybrid and dihybrid crosses.
Interpret human traits and inheritance patterns using pedigree analysis.

topics.title: Non-Mendelian Inheritance
topics.description: Complex patterns of inheritance that deviate from standard Mendelian ratios.
learning_objectives: Analyze outcomes of gene linkage, sex-linked inheritance, and recombination mapping.
Differentiate between codominance, incomplete dominance, polygenic inheritance, epistasis, and pleiotropy.
Explain the role of extranuclear inheritance and environmental influences (epigenetics).

topics.title: Mechanisms of Evolution
topics.description: Sources of genetic variation, natural selection, and Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium.
learning_objectives: Identify sources of genetic variation, including mutation, crossing-over, and horizontal gene transfer.
Explain Darwin-Wallace theories of reproductive fitness and natural selection.
Perform Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium calculations and analyze factors that alter allele frequencies.

topics.title: Population Genetics and Speciation
topics.description: Genetic drift, evolutionary patterns, and the formation of new species.
learning_objectives: Differentiate the effects of gene flow, genetic drift, sexual selection, and artificial selection.
Identify patterns of evolution such as convergent, divergent, coevolution, and adaptive radiation.
Describe the mechanisms of speciation, including reproductive isolation, allopatric, and sympatric speciation.

topics.title: Evidence and Models of Evolution
topics.description: Scientific evidence supporting evolutionary theory and phylogenetic modeling.
learning_objectives: Analyze fossil, biogeographical, homologous, and vestigial evidence supporting evolution.
Explain the molecular evidence for evolution and the endosymbiotic theory.
Interpret evolutionary models such as phylogenetic trees, cladograms, and molecular clocks.

topics.title: Origin of Life and Extinction
topics.description: Theories of early life on Earth and the drivers of species extinction.
learning_objectives: Describe the abiotic synthesis of organic compounds as demonstrated by the Miller-Urey experiment.
Explain the RNA-first hypothesis and biological influences on the early atmosphere.
Identify natural and human-caused factors that lead to the extinction of species.

sections.title: Diversity of Life and Organismal Biology
sections.description: Taxonomy, anatomy, physiology, and evolutionary trends in plants, animals, and microorganisms.

topics.title: Taxonomy and Microorganisms
topics.description: Biological classification and the characteristics of viruses, bacteria, archaea, protists, and fungi.
learning_objectives: Explain the taxonomic hierarchy, domains, kingdoms, and binomial nomenclature.
Identify structural and nutritional characteristics of viruses, bacteria, archaea, protists, and fungi.
Compare unicellular and multicellular organization and modes of reproduction in microorganisms.

topics.title: Animal Evolution and Development
topics.description: Key evolutionary trends in animals, cell specialization, and embryology.
learning_objectives: Describe organizational hierarchy from cells to organ systems and the role of stem cells.
Identify major animal evolutionary trends such as body cavities, symmetry, and cephalization.
Outline animal reproduction, gamete formation, fertilization, and embryonic development patterns.

topics.title: Human Organ Systems
topics.description: Structure and function of the major systems within the human body.
learning_objectives: Describe the functions of the cardiovascular, respiratory, digestive, and excretory systems.
Explain the role of the immune, musculoskeletal, nervous, and endocrine systems.
Trace the flow of blood through the human circulatory system.

topics.title: Animal Homeostasis and Behavior
topics.description: How animals regulate internal environments and display complex behaviors.
learning_objectives: Explain the roles of organs, hormones, and feedback loops in maintaining homeostasis.
Compare endothermic and ectothermic temperature regulation.
Differentiate between innate/learned behaviors, territoriality, and social behaviors like altruism.

topics.title: Plant Characteristics and Structure
topics.description: Plant evolution, tissues, and fundamental anatomy.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between vascular/nonvascular plants and angiosperms/gymnosperms.
Describe the structure and function of dermal, ground, and vascular plant tissues.
Identify the anatomical features and functions of roots, stems, leaves, and flowers.

topics.title: Plant Transport, Reproduction, and Responses
topics.description: Mechanisms of plant transport, life cycles, and environmental adaptations.
learning_objectives: Explain water and nutrient transport via xylem, phloem, and transpiration.
Describe the alternation of generations, gametogenesis, and pollination strategies in plants.
Analyze plant responses to the environment, including tropisms, physical defenses, and major hormones.

sections.title: Ecology: Organisms and Environments
sections.description: Interactions between organisms, population dynamics, biomes, and biogeochemical cycles.

topics.title: Biosphere and Species Interactions
topics.description: Hierarchical structures of ecology and relationships between organisms.
learning_objectives: Define the hierarchical structure of biomes, ecosystems, communities, and populations.
Identify symbiotic relationships including mutualism, parasitism, and commensalism.
Analyze the dynamics of predator-prey relationships, competition, and keystone species.

topics.title: Population Dynamics
topics.description: Models of population growth, demographics, and limiting factors.
learning_objectives: Compare exponential and logistic growth models, identifying carrying capacity.
Differentiate between r-strategist and K-strategist reproductive strategies.
Assess the impact of density-dependent and density-independent limiting factors.

topics.title: Community Structure and Succession
topics.description: Ecological succession, habitat changes, and community resilience.
learning_objectives: Compare primary and secondary ecological succession.
Evaluate changes in biomass, diversity, and productivity during succession.
Analyze the impacts of temporal and spatial disturbances on ecosystems.

topics.title: Ecosystems and Energy Flow
topics.description: Biomes, trophic structures, and the unidirectional flow of energy.
learning_objectives: Describe the defining characteristics of major aquatic and terrestrial biomes.
Construct and interpret food chains, food webs, and ecological pyramids (numbers, biomass, energy).
Explain trophic cascades and the process of biomagnification.

topics.title: Biogeochemical Cycles
topics.description: The cycling of matter through abiotic and biotic ecosystem components.
learning_objectives: Identify the major reservoirs and biotic/abiotic processes of the carbon cycle.
Trace the natural routes of the nitrogen cycle and the phosphorus cycle.
Explain the physical and biological processes that drive the global water cycle.
